replies.author: @hootmanhoot
replies.text: It's a very small island and that becomes apparent once you're there for a while. The people are nice, reserved but welcoming. The native population speaks a cool local language, Papiamento, as described in this video. Aruba is influenced heavily by the Dutch- great food, restaurants and you pay for it. Excellent fresh-water quality via desalination plant. It's a tourist economy for sure. Brutal sun and strong trade winds will not be denied. Minimal rain. I enjoyed being there for my job over an extended period.

comments.author: @lastlivingplaces
comments.text: The 4K cinematography brilliantly captures the unique blend of Caribbean and South American cultures in Aruba. The slow pans make you feel like you are walking the streets alongside the locals. A truly immersive documentary that highlights real life beyond the tourist resorts.

replies.author: @BestTravel2
replies.text: I’m so glad you enjoyed the immersive look at the local life in Aruba!
